content.full_text:
Senate - State of California

SENATE REPUBLICAN CAUCUS
BILL LEONARD, CHAIRMAN
ROBERT J. McKENZIE, Jr., COORDINATOR

March 18, 1992

Mr. John Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This is to confirm today's telephone conversation between you and my secretary, Shirley Seibel, which established that the Political Reform Act does not restrict sending the "Outlook" (copies enclosed) to state political candidates.

The Senate Republican Caucus obtains the articles included in the "Outlook" from various publications, such as newspapers and periodicals (i.e., L.A. Times and Wall Street Journal, and "New Republic", "National Review" and "California Eye").

The Caucus distributes this publication weekly without a cover letter to various individuals throughout the State of California and other states. Those who receive it are members of Republican women's groups, state and federal legislators, constituents, and other private citizens, totaling between 125-150 individuals.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
BILL LEONARD
State Senator

BL:rjs
Enclosures
